task: My mom’s been having a rough week and I’m trying to keep everything straight without panicking. Can you help me make a simple plan for what to watch, what to write down, and when it’s time to call the doctor instead of waiting it out?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely. Here’s a simple, calm plan you can use to keep track of things without feeling overwhelmed.

## 1) What to watch
Keep an eye on these basics:

- **Breathing:** Is she short of breath, wheezing, or breathing faster than usual?
- **Pain:** Where is it, how bad is it, and is it getting worse?
- **Fever:** Any fever, chills, or feeling unusually hot/cold?
- **Eating/drinking:** Is she able to keep fluids down? Is she eating much less than normal?
- **Bathroom changes:** Less urine, diarrhea, constipation, pain with urination, blood, or black stools?
- **Energy/alertness:** More sleepy, confused, dizzy, weak, or hard to wake?
- **Mood/behavior:** More anxious, agitated, withdrawn, or “not herself”?
- **New symptoms:** Rash, swelling, vomiting, falls, headaches, chest discomfort, or anything new.

## 2) What to write down
A simple note on your phone is enough. Try this format:

- **Date/time**
- **Symptom**
- **What you saw**
- **How bad it was** (mild / moderate / severe, or 1–10)
- **What helped or made it worse**
- **Temperature / blood pressure / pulse / oxygen** if you have those
- **Medications taken** and when
- **Food and fluids** if relevant

Example:
- 3/12, 2 PM — headache, 6/10, worse when standing, improved after water and rest. Took acetaminophen at 2:15 PM.

## 3) When to call the doctor
Call her doctor or nurse line **the same day** if:

- Symptoms are **new and not improving**
- She’s getting **worse over 24–48 hours**
- She has **fever**, persistent vomiting, diarrhea, or dehydration signs
- Pain is ongoing or interfering with sleep/function
- She’s confused, unusually weak, or not acting like herself
- You’re unsure whether a medication side effect could be involved

## 4) When not to wait
Get urgent help now / emergency care if she has:

- **Trouble breathing**
- **Chest pain**
- **Fainting**
- **Severe confusion**
- **Stroke signs**: face drooping, arm weakness, speech trouble
- **Severe allergic reaction**: swelling of lips/tongue, hives with breathing trouble
- **Severe bleeding**
- **Severe pain** that’s sudden or intense
- **Blue lips**, seizure, or she’s very hard to wake

## 5) A simple daily routine
Morning / afternoon / evening:
- Check how she looks and sounds
- Ask: “Any new symptoms? Better, worse, or same?”
- Note temperature if needed
- Make sure she’s drinking fluids
- Record meds and any changes

## 6) If you want, use this one-line summary
“**New symptom + how long + how bad + what’s different + any fever/breathing/alertness changes**”

If you want, I can turn this into a **one-page checklist** you can copy into your phone, or help you make it specific to your mom’s actual symptoms.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that’s a decent start, but I need it a little more specific. My mom’s had stroke stuff before, so I’m watching for anything off with her speech, balance, face, or one side going weak. Also if she’s not drinking much, that matters fast. Can you make me a plain checklist I can use morning, afternoon, and night — and a short list of the red flags that mean I call right away?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a plain, easy checklist you can use **morning / afternoon / night**.

## Mom check-in checklist

### 1) Speech
- [ ] Sounds normal
- [ ] Slurred or hard to understand
- [ ] Trouble finding words
- [ ] Confused or not making sense

### 2) Face
- [ ] Face looks even/symmetric
- [ ] One side drooping
- [ ] Uneven smile
- [ ] New trouble closing one eye

### 3) Arms / legs / one-sided weakness
- [ ] Can lift both arms evenly
- [ ] One arm/leg weaker than the other
- [ ] New numbness or tingling on one side
- [ ] Trouble gripping, standing, or walking

### 4) Balance / walking
- [ ] Walking steady
- [ ] More off-balance than usual
- [ ] Stumbling, falling, or needing more help
- [ ] New dizziness or spinning feeling

### 5) Drinking / hydration
- [ ] Drinking normally
- [ ] Drinking less than usual
- [ ] Very little fluid all day
- [ ] Dark urine or not peeing much
- [ ] Dry mouth, very sleepy, or weak

### 6) General
- [ ] Acting like herself
- [ ] More sleepy than usual
- [ ] Confused
- [ ] New headache
- [ ] New chest pain, shortness of breath, or fever

## What to write down
Each time, jot:
- **Time**
- **What changed**
- **Which side if one-sided**
- **Better / worse / same**
- **How much she drank**
- **Any meds taken**

Example:
- **8 AM:** Speech a little slurred, right arm weaker, drank half a glass of water, worse than last night.

## Call right away if you notice any of these
- **Face drooping**
- **Speech changes**
- **One-sided weakness or numbness**
- **New balance trouble, stumbling, or falling**
- **Sudden confusion**
- **Not drinking much / very little urine**
- **Hard to wake up or unusually sleepy**
- **New trouble breathing or chest pain**

## Don’t wait — get emergency help now if
- Symptoms come on **suddenly**
- Any **stroke-like symptom** is new or clearly worse
- She has **trouble speaking, one-sided weakness, or face droop**
- She can’t walk safely
- She’s **too drowsy to wake normally**
- She has **trouble breathing** or **chest pain**

If you want, I can turn this into a **super short phone-note version** you can copy/paste and reuse three times a day.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, that works. Plain is better. I can use this.

The only thing I’d add is if she’s not drinking and her urine gets dark or she’s barely peeing, that’s not something I’d sit on. I’ve seen how fast that can go bad. But the checklist itself is solid.
